data.name: Brandy Claywell
data.history: Born into the small and little known House Claywell, Brandy was taught the importance of the Noble line and what it means to be a true gentleman. Given the proper education and teachings, he grew up with the small luxuries of what a normal Noble would have. Along with the teachings of the great artifacts that were bestowed among the people. And with the artifacts came the Knights, at the right age he was even taught how to use them. But somewhere down the line, politics had gotten to the family. And for reasons unknown to Brandy, the hammer came down on them all. The Claywell name stripped of its Noble ranking, and many of the family either put to death or banished from the lands. Now Brandy walks the lands searching for the reason his family name was tarnished and for ways to bring his noble title back to his name.

frameData.name: Barbarossa
frameData.description:
The Barbarossa is Harrison Armory’s most massive frame to date, built, per the orders of Harrison II, to “stand as the unstoppable image of Harrison I” and to carry the heaviest of weapons and equipment the Armory offers. Standing nearly thirteen meters tall, it is an unsubtle beast of a mech, inspiring terror in enemies and awe in allies. The Barbarossa can mount weapons suitable for engaging toe-to-toe with gunboats and low-gross tonnage subline vessels; due to its size and slow maneuverability, it is often employed in low-gravity engagements where mass is less of a concern.
The Barbarossa is a popular target for Purview essayists, who have been known to remark on the drawbacks that come with its size and how, as a result, it is a perfect stand-in for their political enemies.

traits.name: Heavy Frame
traits.description: The Barbarossa can’t be pushed, pulled, knocked Prone, or knocked back by smaller characters.

active_effects.name: Heavy Frame
active_effects.detail: The Barbarossa can’t be pushed, pulled, knocked Prone, or knocked back by smaller characters.

traits.name: Pressure Plating
traits.description: The Barbarossa has resistance to Explosive Damage.

active_effects.name: Pressure Plating
active_effects.detail: You have Resistance to Explosive damage.

traits.name: Colossus
traits.description: Adjacent allied characters can use the Barbarossa for hard cover.

active_effects.name: Colossus
active_effects.detail: Adjacent allied characters can use the Barbarossa for hard cover.

traits.name: Slow
traits.description: The Barbarossa receives +1 difficulty on Agility checks and saves.

core_system.name: Apocalypse Rail
core_system.description:
Fresh from the Armory’s dayside research and development campus, the LGC-04 “Apocalypse Rail” is an exponential gravitic catapult – a to-scale test product pushed to licensed pilots for field trials as part of the Armory’s ongoing Plenary Beach Magnitude Weapons Test Project.
Distinct from current logarithmic short- or long-spool ship-to-ship weapons (kinetic or energetic/particular), this new platform taps into the Armory’s current gravitic research to exponentially charge and distribute a kinetic payload downrange. This increase in potential energy loading and decrease in total charge time comes at the cost of stability: where the logarithmic spool cannon is stable and shock-resistant, the exponential gravitic catapult is prone to destabilization. For the time being, built-in failsafes will trigger in the event of sudden traumatic destabilization to prevent detonation of the system, resetting any charging process underway at the time of the shock.
The LGC-04 “Apocalypse Rail” system is scaled considerably down from its intended operational role – that is, replacing capital ship spinal spool cannons – in order to effect the accumulation of live data for use in improving the system prior to naval adoption.
core_system.active_effect:
When activated, you start charging the Barbarossa’s Apocalypse Rail, an incredibly powerful ship-to-ship long-spool weapon system that requires target calibration. Gain an Apocalypse Die, 1d6 starting at 4. At the start of each of your turns, lower the value of the Apocalypse Die by 1, to a minimum of 1. If you move (even involuntarily) or become Stunned or Jammed, the Apocalypse Die resets to 4 and then continues to count down as usual.
If the value of the Apocalypse Die is 1–3, you can attack on your turn with the Apocalypse Rail as a full action, but can’t move or take any other actions on the same turn. After an attack with the Apocalypse Rail, the Apocalypse Die resets to 4. If you reach the end of the scene without using it, you regain 1 CP.
At the end of the scene, lose the Apocalypse Die, and the Apocalypse Rail stops charging.

data.name: Pinning Spire

actions.detail: You and one character within Range 3 and line of sight become Immobilized and cannot be moved in any way. For the duration, the target always considers you within range for melee attacks. On any subsequent turn, you can end this effect as a protocol, or your target can end it by successfully hitting you with a ranged or melee attack. This condition can’t be removed any other way.

data.description: “We call it ‘demand-compliance tethering’. Though, that makes it sound as if they have a choice to resist.”

data.name: Reactive Weave
data.effect: When you Brace, you become Invisible until the end of your next turn and may immediately move spaces equal to your Speed.

synergies.detail: When you Brace, you become Invisible until the end of your next turn and may immediately move spaces equal to your Speed.

data.description: Composed of woven covers for critical joints and systems, reactive weave not only protects these sensitive components from fouling and poor weather, but provides a surface for the application of SSC’s unique loomware technology. Reactive weave is powered, making it capable of free-flexing to augment mobility and reduce the stress placed on a mech’s joints.

data.name: Emergency Repair Module
data.description: Emergency repair modules are often a mainstay on Armory chassis galaxy-wide. Through a combination of active nanite slurry, hardpatch welding, and shunting to redundant systems in response to immediate damage, they ensure that a chassis will be able to quickly complete nonmassive battlefield repairs in order to remain operational. A number of informal celebratory traditions have arisen throughout the legions to commemorate a pilot's first time taking damage sufficient to trigger an ERM in combat.
data.effect: Whenever you or an adjacent ally Braces, you may expend a charge and spend 1 Repair to restore that character's HP to full immediately after they take damage from the triggering attack. If that character took structure damage from the triggering attack, they also roll one less die on the structure damage check to a minimum of 0 (which results in no effect).

data.name: Custom Paint Job
data.effect:
When you take structure damage, roll 1d6. On a 6, you return to 1 HP and ignore the damage – the hit simply ‘scratched your paint’.
This system can only be used once before each Full Repair, and is not a valid target for system destruction.

active_effects.name: Custom Paint Job
active_effects.detail: When you take structure damage, roll 1d6. On a 6, you return to 1 HP and ignore the damage – the hit simply ‘scratched your paint’. This system can only be used once before each Full Repair.

data.name: Flak Launcher

actions.detail: Choose a flying character within range 15 and line of sight. They must succeed on an Agility save or immediately land (this counts as falling without any damage), and additionally become Slowed and can’t fly until the end of their next turn.

add_special.detail: You can’t fly until the end of your next turn.

data.description: Designed for use against atmospheric fliers, these anti-air autocannons fire simple, proximity or impact-detonated shells effective against light armor, organic, and subaltern targets.

data.name: Personalizations
data.effect:
You gain +2 HP and, in consultation with the GM, you may establish a minor modification you have made to your mech.
This mod has no numerical benefit beyond the additional HP it grants, but could provide other useful effects. If the GM agrees that this mod would help with either a pilot or mech skill check, you gain +1 Accuracy for that check.

active_effects.name: Personalizations
active_effects.detail: Your customizations relevant to a pilot or mech skill check, gains +1 Accuracy (GM discretion).

data.name: Armament Redundancy
data.effect: You may choose to ignore weapon destruction as a result of the SYSTEM TRAUMA result when taking structure damage. This system can only be used once before each FULL REPAIR, and is not a valid target for system destruction.
data.description: By installing additional armor plating, redundant firing circuits, and enhanced insulation to magazines the longevity of a frame’s armaments can be greatly enhanced.

data.name: Ferrofluid Lance
data.on_hit: 1/round, you may force a character hit by this weapon to make a Hull save. On a failure, both you and your target are Immobilized and cannot be moved in any way. For the duration, the target always considers you within range for melee attacks. On any subsequent turn, you can end this effect as a protocol, knocking your target 4 spaces in any direction, or your target can end it by successfully hitting you with a ranged or melee attack. This condition can’t be removed any other way.

actions.name: Ferrofluid Lance Fling
actions.detail: End the immobilization effect of the Ferrofluid Lance on both you and your target to knock them back 4 spaces in any direction.

data.description: “To say we can pull this needle from ‘thin air’ is the best-fit phrase, but it’s not accurate. Not entirely. There’s no such thing as thin air, not in the way one means it when they use that phrase. ‘Agreeable atomic space’ – that’s what ‘thin air’ really is. A place where one could – if one could – coalesce utility from useless particulate mass with a thought. With a snap of our finger. Here, watch this –”

data.name: Tempest Charged Blade
data.description: The latest innovations of GMS’s Type-II line of powered and energy weapons have produced a charged blade of staggering proportions. It is currently carried in the “Storm” line of superheavy equipment.

data.name: Apocalypse Rail
data.effect:
The Apocalypse Rail must be charged before it can be fired, using the Charge Rail CORE Power.
This weapon has different profiles determined by the current value of the APOCALYPSE DIE when you fire it. Each profile replaces the one proceeding it. The Apocalypse Rail cannot be fired at targets within range 5.
After an attack with the Apocalypse Rail, the Apocalypse Die resets to 4.

actions.name: Fire Apocalypse Rail
actions.detail:
If the value of the Apocalypse Die is 1–3, you can attack on your turn with the Apocalypse Rail as a full action, but can’t move or take any other actions on the same turn.
After an attack with the Apocalypse Rail, the Apocalypse Die resets to 4.

profiles.name: Charge Rail: 3
profiles.effect: Objects within the affected area automatically take 20 AP explosive damage. After the attack, the blast cloud lingers, providing soft cover to characters within the affected area until the end of your next turn.

profiles.name: Charge Rail: 2
profiles.effect: Objects and terrain in the area automatically take 40 AP explosive damage, and on hit characters become Shredded and Impaired until the end of their next turn. The blast cloud is a burning storm: until the end of your next turn, characters within the affected area receive soft cover, and characters that start their turn within the area or move there for the first time in a round take 4 burn.

profiles.name: Charge Rail: 1
profiles.effect: Objects and terrain in the area automatically take 100 AP explosive damage, and characters become Shredded and Stunned until the end of their next turn on hit. The ground in the affected area is vaporized on impact: for the rest of the scene, it is difficult terrain, characters within the affected area receive soft cover, and characters that start their turn with the area or move there for the first time in a round take 4 burn.

data.name: Assault
data.description: Take part in direct and overt combat.
data.detail: Take part in direct and overt combat: fighting your way through a building packed with hostile mercenaries, trading shots between rain-slick trenches, fighting in chaotic microgravity as part of a boarding action, or engaging the enemy in the smoking urban rubble of a city under orbital bombardment — When you assault, you’re always assaulting something (a position, a rival pilot, an enemy force, a group of guards), and it’s always loud, open, direct action.

data.name: Get a Hold of Something
data.description: Acquire temporary or permanent allies, assets, or connections through wealth or social influence.
data.detail: Acquire useful allies, assets, or connections through wealth or social influence. This could be permanent (buying it or receiving it) or temporary (renting or borrowing help or supplies, etc), and might be harder or easier depending on how much you want to use it. This can’t be used for something that’s normally gated by license level (like mech parts) but could be used for aid, supplies, information, food materials, soldiers, or anything else that has more narrative impact. Typically this is acquired by buying it from a market or requisitioning it from a parent organization.

data.name: Pull Rank
data.description: Get information, resources, or aid from a subordinate.
data.detail: Pull rank on a subordinate, getting information, resources, or aid from them, even unwillingly. You can use this on anyone your social status (noble, celebrity, etc) or military rank would have weight with. Failing this might be risky and could be seen as abusive. You typically can’t pull rank on hostile targets. You could also use this to pretend to have a rank you don’t have, but it’s definitely risky.

data.name: Read a Situation
data.description: Look for subtext, motives, or threats in a situation or person.
data.detail: Look for subtext, motive, or threat in a situation or person, often social situations. Use your intuition to learn someone’s motivation, learn who is really in charge, or who is about to do something rash or stupid. Get a gut feeling about a situation or person. Sense if someone is lying to you.

data.name: Stay Cool
data.description: Perform a task that requires concentration, dexterity, speed, or precision under pressure.
data.detail: Do something that requires concentration, speed, or intense precision under pressure, like picking a lock as your squad trades fire with encroaching guards, avoiding a hostile memetic code while hacking a secure console, carefully disarming an explosive, or unjamming a gun under fire. If you’ve got to do something complicated in a high stress situation without messing up (and possibly not even breaking a sweat) this is the action to use.

data.name: Word on the Street
data.description: Get gossip, news, or hearsay from the streets, or from a particular social scene.
data.detail: Get gossip, news, or hearsay from the streets. What you get depends on what ‘streets’ you are getting word from (high society, low society, hearsay, military chatter, etc). This probably takes a lot less time than investigating something in detail, but the information might be more qualitative or colored by opinion (sometimes that might be useful). You can always learn where the information came from or who to go to next.

data.name: Brawler
data.terse: Improve the effectiveness of Improvised Attacks and Grappling
data.description: Up close and personal. The way battle was done since the dawn of time, then forgotten with the first spark of gunpowder. Tempest Gloire is one pilot who prefers the old ways: hand-to-hand, weapons discarded, just the strength of her machine versus the strength of all others. You and her both know that the sweetest victory is found at the culmination of a dance as old as war itself, with the first trick known to humanity: a fist to your enemy’s face.

ranks.name: Hold and Lock
ranks.description: You gain +1 Accuracy on all melee attacks against targets you are Grappling.

ranks.name: Sledgehammer
ranks.description: Your Improvised Attacks gain Knockback 2 and deal 2d6+2 Kinetic damage.

ranks.name: Knockout Blow
ranks.description: Gain a Brawler Die, 1d6 starting at 6. Each time you Grapple, Ram, or make an Improvised Attack, lower the value of the Brawler Die by 1. When the Brawler Die reaches 1, you may reset it to 6 and, as a full action, make a Knockout Blow against an adjacent character. The value of your Brawler Die persists between scenes, but it resets to 6 when you rest or perform a Full Repair.

synergies.detail: Lowers the value of your Brawler Die by 1

actions.name: Knockout Blow
actions.detail:
Reset your Brawler Die to 6 and, as a full action, make a Knockout Blow against an adjacent character. They must pass a Hull save, or take 2d6+2 Kinetic damage and become Stunned until the end of their next turn.
This action can only be taken when the value of your Brawler Die is 1.
actions.condition: The value of your Brawler Die is 1

data.name: Pankrati
data.description: Your fighting is as flashy as the greatest fighters of the Kreoss, and your mech leaps into battle with great aplomb.

ranks.name: Veni
ranks.description: You gain +1 Accuracy to melee attacks against Immobilized or Slowed targets.

synergies.detail: Your melee attacks against Immobilized or Slowed characters gain +1 Accuracy.

ranks.name: Vidi
ranks.description: 1/scene, you can make a Valiant Charge as a free action on your turn, moving up to twice your Speed and ignoring terrain penalties. You must move in a straight line and end your charge adjacent to a hostile character, and you cannot start your charge adjacent to any hostile characters. When you end your charge, any adjacent hostile characters are Slowed until the end of their next turn.

actions.name: Valiant Charge
actions.detail: You charge, moving up to twice your Speed and ignoring terrain penalties. You must move in a straight line and end your charge adjacent to a hostile character, and you cannot start your charge adjacent to any hostile characters. When you end your charge, any adjacent hostile characters are Slowed until the end of their next turn.

ranks.name: Vici
ranks.description: If you start your Valiant Charge adjacent to any allied characters, they may immediately Boost as a reaction after your charge resolves.

data.name: Prospector
data.description: Some people aren’t afraid to get their hands dirty. You are one of these individuals and you aren’t afraid to get your mech’s hands dirty, either. Having equipped it with all sorts of industrial mining equipment, your mech is now able to move great amounts of dirt in a very short amount of time, allowing it and its allies to rapidly redeploy using subterranean routes.

ranks.name: Stake a Claim
ranks.description:
Tunnel: When Tunneling, remove the character from the battlefield and then immediately place them in a free and valid space. This is considered moving 1 space and does trigger Reactions.
Gain the “Create Entrance” Quick Action which you may use 1/Scene.
While completely within an Entrance you and allied characters gain the “Subterranean Reposition” Reaction during your own turns.

actions.name: Create Entrance
actions.detail: 1/Scene While standing on solid ground you may create a Burst 1 Entrance around yourself as a Quick Action. You may then Tunnel to anywhere up to 5 spaces away where you then create another Burst 1 Entrance. Entrances remain in play until the end of the scene. Terrain and sections of terrain overlapping an Entrance take 20 AP Explosive damage. Additionally, you cannot take Reactions until the end of the current turn.

actions.name: Subterranean Reposition
actions.trigger: During your turn, you are completely within an Entrance.
actions.detail: You immediately Tunnel, ending the movement completely within a different Entrance created by the same character as the first.

ranks.name: Gold Fever
ranks.description:
Hostile characters within the area when an Entrance is created must succeed on a Hull save or be knocked Prone.
Additionally, after you use “Create Entrance,” allied characters completely within your initial Entrance may use “Subterranean Reposition” even though it is not their turn.

ranks.name: Mother Lode
ranks.description: “Create Entrance” becomes 1/Round and you may use it an additional time each Scene.  All Entrances you create are connected for the purposes of Tunneling.

data.name: Executioner
data.terse: Improve Heavy and Superheavy Melee attacks with more opportunities to deal damage
data.description: On the battlefield, there is no end more honorable than a clean death in combat. Axe or maul in hand, Maxie Wolf sees to it that her enemies are blessed with that honor. No one lives forever – she makes sure of it.

ranks.name: Backswing Cut
ranks.description: 1/round, when you hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon, you can make another melee attack with the same weapon as a free action against a different character within Threat and line of sight. This attack deals half damage, if successful.

active_effects.name: Backswing Cut
active_effects.detail: 1/round, when you hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon, you can make another melee attack with the same weapon as a free action against a different character within Threat and line of sight. This attack deals half damage, if successful.
active_effects.condition: You hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon

synergies.detail: 1/round, when you hit with this weapon, you can make another melee attack with this same weapon as a free action against a different character within Threat and line of sight. This attack deals half damage, if successful.

ranks.name: Wide Arc Cleave
ranks.description: The first time in a round that you perform a critical hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon, you deal 3 Kinetic damage to all characters and objects of your choice within Threat, other than the one you just attacked.

active_effects.name: Wide Arc Cleave
active_effects.detail: The first time in a round that you perform a critical hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon, you deal 3 Kinetic damage to all characters and objects of your choice within Threat, other than the one you just attacked.
active_effects.condition: The first time you perform a critical hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon

synergies.detail: The first time in a round that you perform a critical hit with this or any other equipped Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon, you deal 3 Kinetic damage to all characters and objects of your choice within Threat, other than the one you just attacked.

ranks.name: No Escape
ranks.description: 1/round, when you miss with a melee attack, you reroll it against a different target within Threat and line of sight.

active_effects.name: No Escape
active_effects.detail: 1/round, when you miss with a melee attack, you reroll it against a different target within Threat and line of sight.
active_effects.condition: You miss with a melee attack

synergies.detail: 1/round, when you miss with a melee attack, you reroll it against a different target within Threat and line of sight.

data.name: Skirmisher
data.terse: Improve your survivability by automatically gaining cover and evading attacks
data.description: What is the best defense? Armor? No. The key to avoiding a messy death in the field, as you learned early, is to stay low, stay mobile, and stay fast. Your mech reflects this philosophy: light, quick, bristling with force-multiplying weapons. You live to push your machine beyond expected parameters, shaking target locks and incoming fire while keeping your own targeting true.

ranks.name: Integrated Chaff Launchers
ranks.description: At the start of your turn, you gain soft cover. You lose this cover if you attack or force another character to make a save.

active_effects.name: Integrated Chaff Launchers
active_effects.detail: At the start of your turn, you gain soft cover. You lose this cover if you attack or force another character to make a save.

ranks.name: Lockbreaker
ranks.description: Before or after you Skirmish (including as a reaction, e.g. from Overwatch), you may move 2 spaces. This movement ignores engagement and doesn’t provoke reactions.

active_effects.name: Lockbreaker
active_effects.detail: Before or after you Skirmish (including as a reaction, e.g. from Overwatch), you may move 2 spaces. This movement ignores engagement and doesn’t provoke reactions.

synergies.detail: Before or after you Skirmish (including as a reaction, e.g. from Overwatch), you may move 2 spaces. This movement ignores engagement and doesn’t provoke reactions.

ranks.name: Weave
ranks.description: The first attack taken as a reaction against you in any round automatically misses.

active_effects.name: Weave
active_effects.detail: The first attack taken as a reaction against you in any round automatically misses.
active_effects.condition: You are targeted by an attack as a reaction

core_bonuses.name: Heatfall Coolant System
core_bonuses.effect: Your cost for Overcharge never goes past 1d6 heat.
core_bonuses.description: The Heatfall Coolant System comes packaged with a stabilized reactor core; paired together, this combo is guaranteed to keep a mech cool in nearly any environment.

reserves.name: Redundant repair
reserves.label: Resource
reserves.description: The ability to STABILIZE as a free action once per mission.

data.name: Assault Hardsuit
data.description: These hardsuits, common among military units, feature heavier plating than light hardsuits but more mobility than heavy hardsuits. They are powered, augmenting the user’s strength, and typically feature an onboard computer, sensor suite, integrated air, burst EVA system, and waste recycling systems.

data.name: Archaic Melee
data.description: These weapons were made using pre-Union alloys and technology, and might be anything from industrial-era steel swords through to stone axes. While they remain widely used on some worlds, archaic weapons used by pilots are usually relics, heirlooms, or ceremonial weapons.

data.name: Archaic Ranged
data.description: These weapons are pre-modern devices like muskets and bows, all of which are still used in some societies.

data.name: Patch
data.effect: Expend a charge to apply a patch to either yourself or an adjacent pilot, restoring half their maximum HP. Patches have no effect on Down and Out pilots.
data.description: “Patch” is pilot slang for any kind of modern first aid gear, including sprayable medi-gel and instant-acting medical patches.

actions.name: Use Patch
actions.detail: Expend a charge to apply a patch to either yourself or an adjacent pilot, restoring half their maximum HP. Patches have no effect on Down and Out pilots.

data.name: Flexsuit
data.description: A strong base-layer suit that recycles water, generates nutrients, and adapts very rapidly to hostile environs, maintaining a stable condition and extending survivability. Flexsuit wearers can go for roughly a week without eating or drink thanks to the ambrosia paste generated by their suit before its systems are depleted; however, they don’t prevent feelings of hunger. Removing the suit for a day or two is enough to replenish its reserves. Flexsuits also maintain a steady temperature within acceptable parameters.

data.name: Extra Rations
data.description: Pilot rations aren’t much better than their nautical forerunners – both are variants on hardtack and nutrient paste. Pilots often carry a stash of extra food, or luxuries like chocolate, coffee, alcohol, or preserved goods from their homeworld. These rations can be used to barter or boost morale.
